meta.title: astro

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: astro
root.mentor_note: astro keeps its eyes on the sky in every word it appears in.

attempt.direction_prompt: Studies the stars, or never looks up at all?

context_sentences: A single childhood evening at the observatory was enough to decide her career in astronomy.
Astronomy had answered most of his questions about the solar system, though the deepest ones remained.
Every astronaut on the mission had trained for this single spacewalk for over a year.
The retired astronaut still speaks, decades later, about the silence outside the capsule window.
Every disputed figure in the report carried a small asterisk pointing to a footnote below.
The editor added an asterisk beside the player’s name, noting the record was set before the rule changed.

construct_options.text: A small star shaped mark used to point to a note or exception
A telescope used to view distant stars
A traveller who journeys through outer space

context_sentences: The astrologer’s column ran in the same corner of the newspaper for nearly forty years.
She consulted an astrologer before every major decision, though she rarely admitted it aloud.

construct_options.text: Someone who studies stars and planets to predict human affairs
A scientist who studies the physical composition of stars
A traveller who has journeyed to another planet
